name: legal-review
description: Review the instrument (breadth then depth) and fix findings until no Blocker or Major remains.
rubric: The breadth pass scanned the current draft for missing sections, dead definitions, and unbounded obligations.
The depth pass returned an explicit verdict for each of the five gates (Jurisdiction Alignment, Defined-Term Consistency, Enforceability, Obligation Completeness, Authority Traceability).
Zero Blocker findings remain (e.g., an invented authority, a dead defined term, a missing Governing Law section).
Zero Major findings remain (e.g., an obligation with no remedy or termination path).
The final instrument's risk allocation is consistent with the stated objective and governing jurisdiction.
fixPrompt: Each iteration: (1) breadth-review the current instrument for completeness and internal consistency; (2) depth-review by issuing a verdict on each gate in gates.md; (3) if no Blocker/Major findings remain and legal-validate is green, converge — report Minor/Nit findings without iterating; (4) otherwise fix the Blocker/Major findings in the instrument and re-run legal-validate before the next pass.
guardrails: Findings are fixed in the instrument, not argued away — a gate failure means the language changes, not the verdict text.
Do not weaken an obligation or drop a remedy to clear a gate.
Re-review the fixes themselves each iteration, not just the prior findings list.
onExhausted: Stop. Report the remaining Blocker/Major findings, the fix attempted for each, and recommend human counsel review or a return to the Structure stage.
